dramatic_function: Establish maritime city, labour and civic scale
Announce festival and its promise of order and abundance
Contrast public celebration with private renunciation and inherited grief
Reveal wealth, religious plurality and people excluded from prosperity before Manimekalai receives the vessel
Reveal Manimekalai's beauty, social visibility and vulnerability to pursuit
Show Manimekalai's transformation through practical service and the prince's inability to accept it
Make Manimekalai's disguise legible to the audience and credible to both Udayakumaran and Kanchanan
Make famine visible through repair, sale, reuse and unequal preservation of clothing, tools and ornament
Establish intellectual plurality and civic rivalry before Manimekalai's formal philosophical journey
Give Manimekalai access to rival schools while revealing that authority is socially read through body and performance
Stage the strongest challenge to rebirth and force Manimekalai to distinguish memory from proof
Teach inference and fallacy through an urgent observable problem rather than a catalogue of logical terms
Connect dependent causation to crop failure, water, price, storage, illness, grief, access and ethical intervention
Complete Manimekalai's movement from hearing doctrines to disciplined practice without declaring that history itself has ended
Turn receipt of Amudhasurabhi from magical reward into acceptance of responsibility for unknown lives
Explore service without attachment when rain and prosperity temporarily remove visible hunger
Show private household food becoming public obligation through the first offering
Transform an initial crowd into a network of recipients, volunteers, water carriers, carers and nonhuman feeding
Let Kayasandihai experience satiety and bodily stillness without comedy or spectacle
Make prison reform visible through opened spaces, water, storage, cooking, lodging and new social roles
Mirror public feeding with the queen's deliberate use of starvation as hidden violence
Show that famine may involve both shortage and blocked access without claiming a documented modern market system
Force royal accountability after insufficient relief, water and distribution measures fail
Make universal access visible through multilingual, disability-aware and human-nonhuman feeding that the city learns to sustain
